name: Google Analytics MCP Server
what_it_does: Google Analytics MCP server enabling AI agents to query GA4 (Google Analytics 4) data — retrieving website traffic metrics, user behavior analysis, conversion tracking, audience segmentation data, and campaign performance analytics via the Google Analytics Data API, integrating web analytics into agent-driven marketing and business intelligence workflows.
use_cases: Querying website traffic and user engagement metrics from analytics agents
Generating automated marketing reports from reporting agents
Analyzing campaign performance and conversion data from marketing agents
Identifying traffic trends and anomalies from monitoring agents
Building custom analytics dashboards from business intelligence agents
Correlating marketing spend with GA4 conversion data from ROI analysis agents
not_for: Universal Analytics (UA was deprecated July 2023 — GA4 only)
Real-time analytics at millisecond resolution (GA4 has data processing delays)
Raw clickstream data (GA4 provides sampled and processed data)
best_when: A marketing or analytics agent needs to query Google Analytics 4 data — for automated reporting, trend analysis, or marketing intelligence workflows using web traffic data.
avoid_when: You need Universal Analytics data (deprecated), real-time clickstream, or raw unsampled event data — use BigQuery GA4 export for raw data.

auth.notes: Google OAuth 2.0 required with Analytics readonly scope. Service Account or OAuth client credentials. GA4 property ID required. Read-only access recommended.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Google Analytics Data API: free with GA4 account; 10,000 requests/day
pricing.paid_tiers: Google Analytics 360 for enterprise with higher limits
pricing.notes: Google Analytics is free for standard use. GA4 Data API is free. Community MCP server is free.

performance.rate_limits: GA4 API: 10,000 requests/day, 1,000 requests/minute for standard accounts

known_agent_gotchas: GA4 data has a 24-48 hour processing delay — real-time dashboard use not recommended
Google OAuth setup for Service Accounts is complex — common auth setup friction
GA4 metrics and dimensions differ from UA — queries for old UA metrics will fail
Data sampling may occur for large date ranges — use shorter ranges for accuracy
GA4 property ID must be configured — different from old UA tracking ID format
Community MCP — GA4 API changes may require updates; check for GA4 API v1 compatibility
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Google OAuth 2.0. Use Service Account with Analytics readonly scope. GDPR considerations for EU user data. Store credentials as environment variables.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. Google OAuth. Service Account with readonly scope. GDPR for EU users. Store credentials securely.
